successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official University of Pennsylvania School of Social Policy & Practice and Penn Vet alumni/profile/news pages, prioritizing person-specific alumni profiles, Q&As, and award announcements with explicit Penn degree-year evidence and substantive post-graduation impact.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Penn SP2 and Penn Vet alumni profile, alumni Q&A, feature, and award pages on sp2.upenn.edu and vet.upenn.edu.

items.name: Chad Dion Lassiter
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Chad Dion Lassiter, MSW’01, was named by President Biden to the Presidential Advisory Commission on Advancing Educational Equity, Excellence, and Economic Opportunity for Black Americans.
items.description: Social work leader and presidential advisory commission appointee.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn SP2 alumni news feature
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-focused news page with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive leadership appointment summary.

items.name: Theresa Alenghat
items.bio: Penn Vet says Theresa Alenghat, V’03, PhD’07, is internationally noted for studying how gut microbes influence health and intestinal disease and now directs a Cincinnati Children’s laboratory focused on microbiome, immune, and epithelial interactions.
items.description: Veterinary scientist and microbiome researcher.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet person-specific alumni profile with explicit Penn degree-year evidence and substantive research-career summary.

items.name: Javier Garcia-Perez
items.bio: Penn SP2 describes Dr. Javier Garcia-Perez, MSW’17, NPL’17, as an interdisciplinary, intersectional, and community-centered researcher focused on improving quality of life for LGBTQ+ and Latinx communities across the lifespan.
items.description: Community-centered researcher focused on LGBTQ+ and Latinx well-being.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni Q&A page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive research summary.

items.name: Ayesha Amin
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Ayesha Amin, MSSP’18, is a feminist activist, gender expert, and founder of Baithak – Challenging Taboos, a women-led grassroots organization advancing gender justice, sexual and reproductive health and rights, and climate justice across Pakistan.
items.description: Gender-justice activist and founder of Baithak.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni Q&A page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive leadership summary.

items.name: Donald Neiffer
items.bio: Penn Vet says Donald Neiffer, V’92, is chief veterinarian for the Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute, leading preventative care, clinical medicine, pathology services, and quarantine programs across the Smithsonian’s animal collections.
items.description: Chief veterinarian at the Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn Vet alumni award announcement
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni award page with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive career-impact summary for an individual honoree.

items.name: Eric Lombardini
items.bio: Penn Vet says Eric Lombardini, C’93, G’01, V’01, is an anatomic pathologist who retired from the U.S. Army as a colonel after directing the Armed Forces Research Institute of Medical Sciences in Thailand and now serves as Boston Scientific’s vice president of pre-clinical global.
items.description: Army medical-research leader and Boston Scientific executive.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn Vet alumni award announcement
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni award page with explicit multi-degree evidence and a substantive career summary for an individual honoree.

items.name: Rosemary Frasso
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Rosemary (Rosie) Frasso, PhD’11, is a health equity researcher, public health educator, and the Victor Heiser, MD Professor of Population Health at Jefferson’s College of Population Health.
items.description: Health equity researcher and population-health professor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni Q&A page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive academic-impact summary.

items.name: Terri Broussard Williams
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Terri Broussard Williams, NPL’20, is an award-winning lobbyist, nonprofit executive, and public speaker who serves as head of state and local government relations at Edward Jones.
items.description: Government-relations executive, lobbyist, and nonprofit leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni Q&A page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive executive-career summary.

items.name: Kerry Doyle
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Kerry Doyle, DSW’22, is an associate professor of practicum education at the USC Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work whose work focuses on trauma-sensitive mindfulness, compassion fatigue among educators, and trauma-responsive interventions.
items.description: Social-work educator focused on trauma-sensitive practice.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni Q&A page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive academic-career summary.

items.name: Meghan Ramos
items.bio: Penn Vet says Meghan Ramos, V’18, is medical director of the Penn Vet Working Dog Center and helps advance veterinary medicine in canine mobility, sports medicine, and performance care.
items.description: Penn Vet Working Dog Center medical director.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni feature page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive person-specific career summary.

items.name: Garrett Harvey
items.bio: Penn Vet says Garrett Harvey, V’18, is director of veterinary affairs for FidoCure, where he oversees clinician education, outreach, and impact research for canine cancer care.
items.description: Veterinary-affairs leader in canine cancer research and therapeutics.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni feature page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive person-specific career summary.

items.name: Jessica Aldrich Strassman
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Jessica Aldrich Strassman, DSW’24, studies expulsion, flight, and migration after researching how World War II trauma narratives are experienced by subsequent generations and continued that work in Greece after graduation.
items.description: Scholar of intergenerational trauma, migration, and displacement.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni Q&A page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive post-graduation research summary.

items.name: Rumyana Kudeva
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Rumyana Kudeva, DSW’15, built an international social work career that took her from Bulgaria to the United States and then to a teaching appointment in Australia.
items.description: International social-work educator and clinical-practice leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni feature page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive international career summary.

items.name: Jennifer A. King
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Jennifer A. King, a 2015 alumna of the DSW program, is a researcher, educator, and leader in trauma-informed systems and the creator of the Cameron Complex Trauma Interview assessment tool.
items.description: Trauma-informed-systems researcher and creator of the CCTI assessment tool.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-specific alumni feature page with explicit alumni-year evidence and substantive research and teaching summary.

items.name: Carla Chieffo
items.bio: Penn Vet says Carla Chieffo, V’86, GR’98, spent two decades leading medical and clinical sciences in early-stage drug development across major pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies and now serves as president of the board of The Spayed Club Veterinary Clinic.
items.description: Veterinarian, biotech leader, and nonprofit clinic board president.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn Vet alumni award announcement
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni award page with explicit multi-degree evidence and a substantive career summary for an individual honoree.

items.name: David Chico
items.bio: Penn Vet says David Chico, V’93, is assistant state veterinarian in the New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets and a longtime leader in animal-health, emergency-preparedness, and veterinary preventive medicine.
items.description: Assistant state veterinarian and animal-health policy leader.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn Vet alumni award announcement
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni award page with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive public-service career summary for an individual honoree.

items.name: Emily A. Conners
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Emily A. Conners, a 2016 graduate of the NPL program, was honored by the Association of Fundraising Professionals Eastern PA Chapter with its Rising Star Award and serves as a development officer at Valley Youth House.
items.description: Nonprofit fundraising leader and AFP Rising Star honoree.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-focused alumni feature page with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantive nonprofit leadership summary.

items.name: Rachel Dungan
items.bio: Penn SP2 says Rachel Dungan, MSSP’13, was named a Fulbright Public Policy Fellow and moved to Côte d’Ivoire to work with the Ministry of Health and Public Hygiene while conducting independent research.
items.description: Fulbright Public Policy Fellow in global public health.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn SP2 person-focused alumni feature page with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive fellowship summary.

items.name: Andrew Nebzydoski
items.bio: Penn Vet says Andrew (Andy) Nebzydoski, V’84, founded Wallenpaupack Veterinary Clinic and has combined a four-decade veterinary career with extensive Penn Vet scholarship, alumni-society, and board service.
items.description: Veterinary clinic founder and longtime Penn Vet volunteer leader.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn Vet alumni award announcement
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni award page with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive service and professional-impact summary for an individual honoree.

items.name: Douglas Antczak
items.bio: Penn Vet says Douglas Antczak, V’73, is the Dorothy Havemeyer McConville Professor of Equine Medicine at Cornell whose interdisciplinary research on the equine placenta has earned international recognition in immunology, genetics, and reproductive biology.
items.description: Internationally recognized equine-medicine researcher and professor.
items.confirmation_label: Official Penn Vet alumni award announcement
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Penn Vet alumni award page with explicit degree-year evidence and a substantive academic-impact summary for an individual honoree.
